headline: Araghchi: We made good progress, it was one of the best and most serious negotiations with the American side
summary: Badr Busaidi, the Minister of Foreign Affairs of Oman, says that the negotiations between Iran and the United States in Geneva, which were held in two sessions in the morning and in the evening, have ended with "significant progress". According to the Omani negotiator, the two sides will resume negotiations soon after consulting in their respective capitals. He also said that technical level talks will be held in Vienna next week. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi also said after the end of the third round of negotiations that "we made good progress" and "this was one of our best and most serious negotiations with the American side." A senior American official also told Axios that the talks were "positive".

headline: The Taliban government says it has attacked targets on the border with Pakistan
summary: The Taliban government has announced that it has started a military operation against the positions of the Pakistani army along the border between the two countries.

headline: A court in France sentenced Mahdia Esfandiari to one year in prison on the charge of "justifying terrorism".
summary:
On Thursday, a court in France sentenced Mahdia Esfandiari, an Iranian citizen, to one year in prison on charges of "justifying terrorism".
Some experts believe that Ms. Esfandiari's case is tied to the case of two French citizens in Iran.

headline: from Lebanon to Yemen; Will the possible war between Iran and America activate the "Axis of Resistance"?
summary: For decades, the Islamic Republic of Iran pursued the "War in the Gray Zone" strategy to expand its deterrence by relying on a network of regional armed actors, but after October 7, 2023, and the expansion of Israel's attacks on this network and even targets inside Iran, Tehran faced increasing pressure to choose between retreating or escalating tensions.

headline: Cuba's fatal shooting of a speedboat registered in the United States; Havana says the occupants had "terrorist intentions."
summary:
Cuba has accused the 10 passengers of a US-registered speedboat it intercepted near its coast on Wednesday of "infiltrating with terrorist purposes".
The Cuban Ministry of Interior announced in a statement that border guards responded to the shooting of the boat occupants, killing four of them and wounding six others.
